rulings.Case Title: STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA VS. DAVID REPLOGLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Order Authorizing State Bar Of California
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 26, 2012, Line 3, PLAINTIFF STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's Application For Order Authorizing State Bar Of California. The State Bar possesses documents obtained after it took over the practice of an erstwhile lawyer, Replogle (Replogle Documents). The Replogle Documents may include things subject to attorney-client privilege and may relate to a pending suit in the North District of California, Roe v. White (03-04035) . The Replogle Documents likely also include many items not related to that case. The State Bar seeks instruction from this Court on how to respond to what is in effect a request from the federal court to provide the Replogle Documents for use in that case. Magistrate Judge Cousins has been directed to review any produced documents in a reference order of May 18, 2012 (Reference Order). The Reference Order in turn cites a stipulated protective order of November 15, 2011 which provides for the confidential treatment of certain papers and things (Protective Order). The Reference Order directs Judge Cousins to examine documents "defined in paragraph 2" of the Protective Order, but Paragraph II (there is not one literally denominated "2") does not contain such a definition. However, Paragraph I plainly contemplates a judicial review of the Replogle Documents, and I understand the Order is further limited to those documents which pertain to Roe v. White. Protective Order at 1 line 6 (re: case "against Defendant"). Thus the only documents Judge Cousins could possibly release to anyone would Replogle Documents related to Roe v. White. I further understand it that if Judge Cousin found through an in camera inspection that Roe v White documents were protected by an attorney-client privilege, those things would not be disclosed (except to the clients and their counsel) but instead would be noted in a privilege log. Thereafter, of course, federal litigation may take place in order to resolve an asserted claim of attorney client privilege. Under these circumstances, and assuming any document not relating to Roe v. White will be returned to the State Bar, the State Bar is authorized to provide the Replogle Documents to Judge Cousins. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MICHAEL MURRAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-508323 WITH CASE CGC-09-494591
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 26, 2012, Line 4, CROSS DEFENDANTs JAMES NUNEMACHER, THE CHRIS JAMES CORPORATION's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-508323 WITH CASE CGC-09-494591. Granted. There are common issues of fact and law as among the claims and defenses in the proposed cases. There are sufficient commonalities of parties, likely witnesses, overlap of counsel, and issues, to demonstrate that consolidation is the most efficient means to handle pre-trial matters. The trial judge retains the discretion to determine the order of proof, and whether claims or defenses should be severed for trial purposes, including the severance of claims for earlier trial. The parties may wish to apply for assignment of this case to a single judge for all purposes. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: JASON WONG VS. BURLINGAME PROFESSIONAL BUILDING, INC., A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memo Of P & A; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 26, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT BURLINGAME PROFESSIONAL BUILDING, INC.s motion For Judgment On The Pleadings and request for judicial notice. First cause of action (contract): Granted. Plaintiff has not allege a contract as to which BPB is a party. Third cause of action (fraud): Granted. Plaintiff has not alleged which person was authorized to make representations on behalf of BPB or which person at BPB concealed the facts, all done prior to the parties' consent to the proxy/management control agreement (which person could not, therefore, have been G. Wong). Fourth cause of action (concealment): Granted, same as with third cause of action. Causes of action 8, 9 and 10: Granted: Plaintiff did not seek leave to amend. The Court must and will ignore the request for leave contained in plaintiff's opposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WINFIELD DESIGN INTERNATIONAL, INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO PLANNING DEPARTMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 26, 2012, Line 12, RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Overruled. To the extent the first Amended Petition (FAP) alleges a rule previously determined by the Court to be time barred (e.g. 9), Petitioner is unable to proceed. But the FAP has other allegations in effect claiming that Defendant wrongfully penalized ($250/day) Petitioner for evicting a tenant, whereas (the FAP claims) the tenant was not evicted. The requisite abuse of discretion standard for a writ of mandamus may be established by e.g. an order or decision not supported by findings or findings not supported by evidence. Accordingly a claim is stated and the demurrer cannot be sustained. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS MOLLOY VS. BLUE SHIELD, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Change In Date Of Hearing On Mtn To Protect Third Party Witness Dr. Mary Scheltheis And Other Witnesses From Harassment Or Intimidation By Pltf; Req. For Monetary Sacntions In The Amount Of $5650.00 Against Dennis Malloy And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 26, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD'S MOTION AND Change In Date Of Hearing On Mtn To Protect Third Party Witness Dr. Mary Scheltheis And Other Witnesses From Harassment Or Intimidation By Pltf; Req. For Monetary Sacntions In The Amount Of $5650.00 Against Dennis Malloy - Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion denied. The evidence submitted in support of the Motion does not show that Mr. Molloy tampered with or intimidated the witness. The doctor's deposition testimony shows that she agreed with Mr. Molloy that she should perform the takedown surgery, while also admitting that, while not ideal, any competent surgeon could do the procedure. In part of the deposition testimony not highlighted by the moving party, the Doctor was asked if the letter from Molloy at issue here was intimidating. Her answer: "Not after reading it again. I mean, it's -no." Ex. F, Depo. Transcript, 157:18-19. For this and several other reasons, the motion is borderline frivolous. No sanctions are awarded. Plaintiff did not cite any law or provide any competent evidence to support his request for sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: REGULO SIERRA VS. STEVEN KREMER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Reconsideration Of Ruling Of Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 26, 2012, Line 16, PLAINTIFF REGULO SIERRA's motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling Of Demurrer. Denied. Plaintiff does not present any new law or facts upon which to base the reconsideration motion. The motion fails to comply with CCP § 1008. Plaintiff's motion to add Judge Kahn as a doe defendant is also denied. It appears that Mr Sierra's complaint with Judge Kahn is that Judge Kahn decided earlier motions incorrectly; judicial immunity bars such claims in any event. Mr Sierra also appears to make a motion to disqualify Judge Kahn under CCP § 170.6. While that is moot for this motion, decided by a different judge, the motion is denied as untimely, brought long after Mr Sierra learned Judge Kahn would hear matters in this case. Mr. Sierra is reminded that his papers are excessive in length. See California Rules of Court 3.1103 (d)(15 pages for opening memorandum and 20 pages for reply papers). I have in any event reviewed these papers, but in the future the Court may not review papers which are excessive in length.. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice per Local Rule 2.6B. No courtesy copies of moving and reply papers were provided to the court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Striking Out Defts Answer, Rendering Judgment By Default Against Deft'S And For Monetary Sacntions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 26, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS MARK MIGDAL, MARK MIGDAL AS TRUSTEE OF THE MARK MIGDAL 2000'S MOTION For Order Striking Out Defts Answer, Rendering Judgment By Default Against Defendants And For Monetary Sanctions - Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court has reviewed all the pleadings including the Reply Brief and Declaration. The Motion is denied on the basis that the Opposition and Declaration in Support contends that all documents have been produced and the Reply Declaration does not specifically refute that contention. It states only that on June 15, 2012 Plaintiff informed counsel that the documents had not been produced. The parties may wish to appear in order to clarify whether or not the documents have been produced. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
